title: EPHEMERAL Conformance Vector Suite Schema
description: JSON Schema defining the file format for EPHEMERAL conformance test vector files. Vector files are JSON for source form; implementers serialize to deterministic CBOR at consumer time per RFC 8949 §4.2.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema definition. Vectors compatible with 1.0.0 will remain parseable by 1.x validators.

vector_suite.description: Which of the eight conformance suites this file represents. `anomaly-library-reject` was added in Phase C.4 Session 4 for AnomalyPatternLibrary envelope verification (design-final.md §3.5). `anomaly-detect` was added in Phase C.4 Session 5-B for firing-rule evaluation against a verified library (design-final.md §3.5.3, §11.2).

spec_version.description: Git commit hash or version tag of the design-final.md at the time vectors were authored.

coverage_summary.description: Optional structured summary of what categories are covered and how many vectors per category.

id.description: Stable identifier. Prefix matches the vector suite (e.g., 'ds-' for delegation-scope, 'canon-' for canonicalization, 'fuzz-' for fuzz-baseline, 'trej-' for tariff-reject, 'pcrrej-' for pcr-attestation-reject, 'arep-' for audit-replay). Zero-padded sequence.

category.description: Short category tag, kebab-case. Categories group vectors by attack class or spec-ambiguity area. Use consistently within a file.

description.description: One-line human-readable description of what this vector tests.

input.description: The input payload the implementation under test receives. Shape is suite-specific; see per-suite README.

reject_code.description: Machine-readable reject reason. Present if outcome is 'reject'. Suite-specific enum; see per-suite README.

output.description: Expected output object for suites where outcome='accept' produces a value (e.g., canonicalization emits a canonical form; fuzz-baseline emits a tier).

rationale.description: Cite spec section or redteam finding that motivated this vector. This is the trace that proves coverage.

redteam_refs.description: Optional list of redteam finding IDs (e.g., 'V3-1', 'V2-2') that this vector defends against.

severity_if_failed.description: Severity of an implementation failing this vector. CRITICAL = would ship a security bug; HIGH = would ship a correctness bug that could be exploited; MEDIUM = correctness issue without immediate exploit; LOW = cosmetic or ergonomic.
